_comment: Drives the Mock ERP Generator (03_mock_erp_generator.py) end to end. Nothing about scenario mix, mutation behavior, or randomness lives in the notebook -- it all reads from here. Percentages are targets applied to the Silver VENDOR_STATEMENT population for this vendor/period; the generator rounds to whole invoices and logs the exact assignment to the mutation manifest.

exact_match.description: ERP record mirrors the statement exactly -- vendor, invoice number, amount, RO, shop all agree.

invoice_revision.description: ERP re-issues the invoice number with a revision suffix; the statement keeps the original. Validates the normalization function (src/normalization.py) resolves this at Level 1.

missing_invoice.description: Invoice exists on the vendor statement but is entirely absent from the ERP extract -- expected exception reason: Invoice Missing.

amount_mismatch.description: ERP amount differs from the statement amount by a random percentage in this range -- expected exception reason: Amount Mismatch.

duplicate_invoice.description: The same invoice appears more than once in the ERP extract -- expected exception reason: Duplicate Invoice.

vendor_reference_issue.description: ERP references the work order number instead of the true invoice number -- mirrors the real SIN12307276 / 24419074 case found during Phase 2 extraction. No RO number is carried on this record, so Level 3 cannot rescue it either -- expected exception reason: Invoice Missing.

missing_credit.description: ERP outstanding amount is reduced by a credit that never appears on the vendor statement -- expected exception reason: Missing Credit.

pending_posting.description: Invoice exists on the ERP side but has not finished posting yet -- expected exception reason: Pending Posting.
